self_improvement.claim: Directive review and lifecycle loops will drift less because lifecycle and score-feedback behavior now exists as one canonical helper.
self_improvement.mechanism: Lifecycle transition validation, review-score deltas, and blocked recovery planning are now implemented in one product-owned shared lib.
self_improvement.baseline_observation: Directive Workspace had lifecycle and score-feedback contracts but no canonical executable helper implementing them.
self_improvement.expected_effect: Later review-oriented Architecture work can import one helper instead of re-deriving transition and score logic from prose.
self_improvement.verification_notes: The helper is now consumed by the Architecture review-resolution lane.

decision.rationale: The mechanism passed review, met adoption readiness, and remains valuable as Directive-owned Architecture output.
decision.forge_threshold_check: yes - the mechanism is still valuable without a runtime surface, so Architecture should retain product-owned value
